title: Community-Owned Data Centers - The 'Impossible' Framework
impossibility_claims: Too technically complex for communities to manage
Too expensive for local groups to afford
Requires specialized expertise only corporations have
Security risks if not centrally controlled

realities.technical: Many communities already run complex infrastructure (water, power, hospitals)
realities.financial: Cooperative ownership models work in multiple sectors
realities.expertise: Technical talent exists outside corporate centers
realities.security: Distributed systems are more secure than centralized ones
realities.reliability: Local control enables faster issue response

why_they_fear: AI maintained by communities with beneficial goals
No corporate kill switches or forced compliance
Democratic governance instead of corporate control
Profit motive eliminated from AI development
actual_solution: Local renewable energy + community ownership + distributed AI = independence from centralized control

pad.source: inferred from keyword analysis of sensor content
pad.reference: Rosetta Shape Core generate.py — PAD compression layer
